# Copilot Instructions ‚Äî DOOM (Linux 1.10) Modernization

> **Generated by the `arch:modernize` skill as an editable starting point.**
> This is the path GitHub Copilot auto-loads. It encodes the *canonical commands*
> and *phase-gating rules* for executing `MODERNIZATION_PLAN.md`. Tune to taste
> and delete this note once customized.

DOOM (Linux 1.10) is being **modernized**. The 1997 toolchain is **dead** (X11
8-bit `PseudoColor`, OSS `/dev/dsp`, removed headers like `values.h`), but the
damage is contained to the `i_*` platform layer ‚Äî the portable core (`r_*`,
`p_*`, `g_*`, `w_`, `z_`) is near-compilable. The safety strategy is
**adaptive**: each component sits on the **highest achievable rung of the safety
ladder (L0‚ÄìL4)**, has a named **Testability Milestone** (the core crosses it at
the end of **Phase 2**, the SDL beachhead), and every phase is labeled
**pre-testability ("dark")** or **post-testability ("lit")**. See
`MODERNIZATION_PLAN.md` for the phase roadmap, per-component testability/rung,
and residual-risk register; `ARCHITECTURE.md` for the audited current state. Work
one phase at a time; do not advance until the current phase's exit criteria are
demonstrably met.

## Commands

> Today the repo has **only `make`** ‚Äî no tests, linter, formatter, typecheck, or
> CI (`ARCHITECTURE.md` Commands & Verification Inventory). The rows below marked
> *(introduced in Phase N)* do not exist yet; they are created as the plan
> executes. Keep this table current as each phase lands.

| Action | Command |
|--------|---------|
| Build (legacy, Linux-only, current) | `cd linuxdoom-1.10 && make` ‚Üí `linux/linuxxdoom` |
| Build sound server (legacy, *retired Phase 4* ‚Üí `legacy/sndserv/`) | `cd legacy/sndserv && make` *(archived; no longer part of the modern build)* |
| Clean | `cd linuxdoom-1.10 && make clean` |
| Build (modern, *introduced Phase 1*) | `cmake -B build && cmake --build build` |
| Run / play | `DOOMWADDIR=<dir-with-doom1.wad> ./build/doom` *(IWAD is found by hard-coded filename via `DOOMWADDIR`, **not** `-iwad`; symlink `freedoom1.wad`‚Üí`doom1.wad`; in-process SDL audio on by default (Phase 4), `-nosound`/`-nosfx` to silence)* |
| Run a demo (oracle) | copy `tests/fixtures/parity.lmp` into a rundir with `doom1.wad`, then `DOOMWADDIR=<rundir> HOME=<rundir> ./build/doom -playdemo parity` |
| Demo-parity test (Phase 2 ‚úÖ) | `ctest --test-dir build -R demo-parity` *(replays scripted v110 demo, asserts self-frozen world-state checksum `a00552bbf22274a2`)* |
| Frame-hash smoke (Phase 2 ‚úÖ) | `ctest --test-dir build -R frame-smoke` *(byte-exact indexed `screens[0]` hash `3e61b0f0c5dfd943` + non-blank guard)* |
| Palette-LUT gate (Phase 2 ‚úÖ) | `ctest --test-dir build -R palette-lut` *(validates `I_SetPalette` index‚ÜíARGB8888 + gamma)* |
| Demo-regen check (Phase 2 ‚úÖ) | `ctest --test-dir build -R demo-regen` *(asserts `tools/gen_demo.py` reproduces `parity.lmp` byte-exact)* |
| Full parity gate (Phase 3 ‚úÖ) | `ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure` *(all 5 targets; what CI runs)* |
| Interactive-play smoke (Phase 3 ‚úÖ) | manual ‚Äî see `docs/interactive-play-checklist.md` *(needs a desktop session; `-grabmouse` boot recipe + keyboard/mouse step table)* |
| Audio smoke (Phase 4 ‚úÖ) | manual ‚Äî see `docs/audio-smoke-checklist.md` *(needs a desktop + audio device; L1 perceptual SFX-in-sync checklist; oracle/`-nosound` modes suppress audio)* |
| Loopback net test (Phase 5 ‚úÖ) | `ctest --test-dir build -R net-loopback` *(2 real `doom` procs over 127.0.0.1 UDP via `platform/posix/i_net_posix.c`; scripted per-node input; asserts lockstep held (no consistency failure) + self-frozen 2-player checksum `e8ca533e8baf4ad4`)* |
| Lint / Format / Typecheck | **none** ‚Äî rely on `-Wall -Wextra` (and `-Werror` on platform files, Phase 3 ‚úÖ) |
| Package a release locally | `./tools/package_release.sh <version> [build-dir] [out-dir]` *(builds an installable app: macOS `.dmg` (DOOM.app, drag-to-Applications) or Linux `.AppImage`; bundles engine + vendored SDL2 + Freedoom `doom1.wad` + icon + licenses; launcher points `DOOMWADDIR` at read-only data and `HOME`+cwd at a per-user data dir; macOS ad-hoc-signs; fails on any leaked build/Homebrew path)* |
| Cut a downloadable release | **Push a tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z origin/main && git push origin vX.Y.Z`** ‚Üí publishes a public release. *(The Actions ‚Üí "Release" ‚Üí Run workflow button is a **draft dry-run** only ‚Äî no git tag, no public download URLs.)* |

CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, Phase 3 ‚úÖ) builds on **Linux + macOS** and runs
`build ‚Üí ctest` (the full 5-target parity gate, incl. `net-loopback` Phase 5 ‚úÖ)
on every push to `main`, every PR, and `workflow_dispatch`. It is **not** yet a
*required* status check ‚Äî enforcement is a manual GitHub-UI step (Settings ‚Üí
Branches ‚Üí protect `main`).

## Releases

Downloadable macOS `.dmg` + Linux `.AppImage` builds are published by
`.github/workflows/release.yml`. **`main` is branch-protected ‚Äî you never push
commits to it.** A **tag** is a separate ref from the `main` branch, so branch
protection does **not** block it. Trigger a release from already-merged,
CI-green `main`:

**Push a version tag (the only path that publishes a public release):**

```
git tag v1.0.0 origin/main     # tag the current merged main tip
git push origin v1.0.0         # pushes only the tag ref, not the branch
```

On a tag push the workflow sets `draft=false` and `softprops/action-gh-release`
attaches the assets to the now-existing tag ‚Üí a public, downloadable release.

**Dry-run only ‚Äî the button:** GitHub ‚Üí **Actions** ‚Üí **Release** ‚Üí **Run
workflow** (branch `main`, version `vX.Y.Z`) builds + packages, but publishes a
**draft** with **no git tag** (asset download URLs 404 until published). Use it
to inspect artifacts, not to ship. Delete the draft afterward.

Either path runs the workflow: build on `ubuntu-22.04` + `macos-latest`, run the
**full parity gate** (a red gate blocks the release), package each platform's
installable app via `tools/package_release.sh` (macOS `.dmg`, Linux
`.AppImage`), **smoke-boot the packaged app headlessly** (mount+launch the
`.dmg`; `--appimage-extract-and-run` the AppImage), attach a GPLv2
corresponding-source archive (`doom-<version>-source.tar.gz`), and publish the
GitHub Release. Only a pushed `v*` tag publishes a public release; the manual
button produces a **draft dry-run** (no tag, no public download URLs). Versioning
(semantic `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`): bump **PATCH** for fixes, **MINOR** for new
user-visible capability, **MAJOR** for a break in the WAD/demo data contract or
CLI. Use `-rcN`/`-beta` suffixes for pre-releases.
Full checklist + release-notes template: `docs/RELEASING.md`.

> **Never invent a command you haven't verified.** The only command verified
> against the current tree is `make` in `linuxdoom-1.10/` and `legacy/sndserv/`
> (the latter archived Phase 4), plus `cmake`/`ctest` for the modern build.

## Phase gating (regime-aware; applies to every phase)

A phase is **not complete** until its **Verification & Exit Criteria** in
`MODERNIZATION_PLAN.md` are:

1. **Objectively verifiable** ‚Äî runnable commands / captured evidence, not a
   subjective "looks done".
2. **Actually executed and recorded** ‚Äî run them, note the result in the plan's
   phase-status tracker before starting the next phase.
3. **Gated** ‚Äî do **not** advance to phase N+1 until phase N's criteria pass.

**Which criteria are valid depends on the component's regime:**

- **Post-testability ("lit") phases** (Phase 2 exit onward): exit on runnable
  commands / **green CI on the phase's branch/PR ‚Äî the authoritative signal.**
  Shared baseline: the build must succeed and **demo-parity must stay green**; if
  the change touched video/audio/net or any `i_*.h` contract, the relevant
  frame-hash / net-loopback checks must also stay green.
- **Pre-testability ("dark") phases** (Phases 0, 1, and Phase 2 entry): the
  engine isn't alive yet, so a green test gate is a **category error**. Exit on
  the phase's **safety-ladder rung** instead ‚Äî captured oracle/seam snapshots
  (demo checksum, frame hashes), reversibility scaffolding (atomic commits,
  CMake builds), a passed smoke checklist. **Do not block a dark phase on a test
  suite that can't run yet.** A blessed **downgrade** to a lower rung (audio L1,
  DOS drivers L0) with residual risk named is a valid exit, not a failure.

**Never require a component's automated test gate before it crosses its own
Testability Milestone** (the core crosses at Phase 2; audio at Phase 4; net at
Phase 5). Report to the user any phase whose pass/fail is unknown rather than
assuming it passed.

## Decisions

- **No external source port ‚Äî build the oracle with what we have.** Treat this as
  if **no ported/modern DOOM exists** (no Chocolate Doom, Crispy, PrBoom, DSDA,
  etc.): do **not** install, run, or borrow demos/frames/checksums from any
  external reimplementation, and do **not** stand up a period-correct legacy
  binary. The correctness oracle is layered and fully self-contained
  (`docs/oracle/ORACLE_STRATEGY.md`): **(L1)** static seam/data contracts captured
  in Phase 0 from the WAD bytes + source, and **(L3)** a **self-frozen** golden
  master ‚Äî our *own* engine records a native demo via `-record` at the end of
  Phase 2, and its consistency checksum + frame hashes become the reference every
  later lit phase diffs against. The residual risk (the *initial* port can't be
  independently blessed, only self-consistency of later refactors) is named and
  accepted ‚Äî do not "fix" it by reaching for an external port.
- When a phase is planned, **all sub-decisions are resolved and documented** in
  `MODERNIZATION_PLAN.md` so implementation needs no further user input.
- State **"dropped" vs "deferred"** explicitly. Already decided: language stays
  **C11**; backend is **SDL2**; build is **CMake**; renderer/playsim are
  **kept**; DOS `ipx/`+`sersrc/` are **dropped/archived**; separate `sndserver`
  is **dropped**; the lockstep netcode is **ported to a raw POSIX BSD-sockets
  transport** (`platform/posix/i_net_posix.c`, **Linux+macOS only, no SDL_net /
  no new deps**, Phase 5 ‚úÖ); GPU renderer and a rearchitected client/server
  netcode are **deferred**.
- Genuine stakeholder blockers live in the plan's ¬ß9 Open Questions (CI IWAD
  licensing, Windows scope, GPU-renderer direction) ‚Äî resolve with the user, do
  not guess.
- Update the plan's **phase-status tracker** (‚òê/‚úÖ complete / ‚è≠Ô∏è descoped /
  üóëÔ∏è dropped) and the **¬ß3.4 residual-risk register** as components cross their
  Testability Milestone.

## Branching & PRs

Each phase is developed on its **own branch** ‚Äî never commit phase work directly
to `main`. Create a branch at the start of a phase (e.g.
`phase-3-ci-interactive`). Once exit criteria are met and recorded, push and
open a PR to **`main`** (the default branch). For **lit** phases, green CI on the
PR is the authoritative signal before merge. For **dark** phases (0, 1, Phase 2
entry), the PR instead carries the achievable rung's evidence ‚Äî captured demo
checksum / frame hashes, CMake-builds-clean, smoke-checklist results ‚Äî with
residual risk named. *(CI runs on PRs but is not yet a required status check ‚Äî
enforcing it on `main` is a manual GitHub-UI step.)*

Post-modernization work (docs, packaging, releases) follows the same
branch-then-PR rule. **Releases are tagged from `main` only**, after the PR is
merged and CI is green ‚Äî never from a feature branch (see "Releases" above).
